summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a short but superb 1986 gig – highlights include High Time, Comes A Time and a tight Scarlet/Fire.
review: The Miller soundboard recording is remarkably clear and well‑balanced, letting Jerry’s surprisingly crisp vocals and backing harmonies shine.  Fans rave about the sweet High Time, an emotionally resonant Comes A Time and the energetic Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence, while the first set’s tight song selection keeps the momentum high despite the show’s brief length.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen reviews, most giving 4‑5 stars, praise both performance and sound quality; only minor criticisms (short show length, occasional sloppy moments) keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with good instrument separation; reviewers note “superior recording” and “best qual” – no major drop‑outs or muffiness reported.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: Short (≈2 ½ hrs) but tightly curated; an excellent first set with strong tune selection, a high‑energy second set featuring jam‑heavy moments and a memorable encore‑less finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as clear, crisp, sweet and full of harmony; backing vocals on "The Race Is On" highlighted as outstanding.
band_member_comments.Phil: Only brief mention (talking to the Heads); no specific performance comment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for central jams on Sugar Magnolia; occasional slip noted on "Promised Land" where his lyrics were muffed.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mid‑80s Brent (keyboard) era praised as “MVP territory”; keyboards felt strong throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums segment noted as having more going on than usual, adding excitement to the second set.
